subject: Chemistry
title: Lavoisier Names Oxygen
description: A quick win: understand Lavoisier Names Oxygen.

hook.text: Antoine Lavoisier didn't discover oxygen, but he figured out what it actually does—and named it.

buildup.text: Joseph Priestley isolated the gas in 1774. He called it 'dephlogisticated air.' Nobody understood it.

discovery.text: Lavoisier proved this gas was essential for burning and breathing. He named it oxygen: 'acid-maker.'

twist.text: The name was actually wrong. Oxygen doesn't make acids—hydrogen does. But the name stuck.

climax.text: Lavoisier's work killed the phlogiston theory and created modern chemistry's first element list.

punchline.text: Named it wrong, understood it right. Chemistry was born.

quiz.question: What did Lavoisier prove about oxygen?
options: It is essential for combustion and respiration
